file_name: Wolswijk et al. - 2025 - The impact of clinical and dermoscopic photographs on the diagnostic accuracy of remote optical cohe.json

did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The study did not use AI, machine learning, or deep learning. It was a comparative diagnostic study where four human OCT assessors (one expert and three regular assessors) manually assessed optical coherence tomography (OCT) scans with and without clinical/dermoscopic photographs. Diagnostic accuracy was evaluated by comparing human assessments against histopathology reference standards.

non_compliance_reason: The paper does not use AI to obtain results in dermatology.
